generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures real-time temperature data with high precision for calibration of thermal models in the Digital Twin. Supports event-triggered sampling for anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time pressure measurements from critical components to validate structural integrity models and detect operational anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors dynamic vibrations across system components using high-frequency sampling (10-100Hz) for predictive maintenance and fault detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks real-time positional data of moving components with millimeter-level accuracy for kinematic modeling and trajectory validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionDataTransmitted
]

instance VelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures instantaneous velocity data for dynamic system analysis and calibration of motion models in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance ForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Quantifies applied forces across system interfaces with high-resolution sampling for structural analysis and load distribution modeling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ForceDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects environmental parameters (weather, EMI) to contextualize physical system behavior and improve model accuracy under varying conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance CalibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides reference measurements for dynamic calibration of sensor networks and model parameters using least squares estimation techniques."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CalibrationDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance PressureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

instance PositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSensor
]

instance VelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VelocitySensor
]

instance ForceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ForceSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance CalibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CalibrationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based representation of physical components capturing spatial attributes and structural geometry for visualization and collision detection."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GeometricModelingEngine
]

instance DynamicBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential equation-based model describing system dynamics including forces, motion, and environmental interactions for predictive analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEngine
]

instance AnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical formulation of physical laws embedded in the Digital Twin for predictive and analytical purposes including stress analysis and load distribution."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationEngine
]

instance SensorCalibrationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Parameter estimation model using least squares and Bayesian filtering techniques to dynamically adjust sensor readings and model parameters."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationEngine
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical repository for time-series sensor data with timestamping and provenance tracking for model validation and trend analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, PressureDataTransmitted, VibrationDataTransmitted, PositionDataTransmitted, VelocityDataTransmitted, ForceDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEngine
]

instance StructuredMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata repository containing sensor calibration parameters, data provenance, and validation results for traceability and quality assurance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CalibrationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance OpcUaGateway : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Industrial standard protocol gateway enabling secure, interoperable data exchange between physical sensors and Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance EthernetIpNetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-speed, deterministic industrial network protocol supporting real-time data transmission for dynamic system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSimulationService, ControlService
]

instance MqttBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker enabling decoupled communication between distributed sensor networks and Digital Twin processing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingEngine, FaultDetectionService
]

instance TimescaleDb : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for storing and querying high-frequency sensor data with sub-second precision."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService, HistoricalAnalysisService
]

instance KalmanFilterEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation engine combining multiple sensor inputs using Kalman filtering algorithms for noise reduction and coherent state reconstruction."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService, PredictionService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics simulation engine solving differential equations to reproduce and predict Physical Twin behavior under various conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService, ConsequenceAnalysisService
]

instance CalibrationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Parameter estimation engine applying least squares and Bayesian filtering techniques to dynamically adjust model parameters using real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ParameterCalibrationService, ModelRefinementService
]

instance OptimizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Mathematical optimization engine applying gradient descent and constrained solvers for real-time parameter tuning and performance optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService, ControlService
]

instance DataProcessingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Preprocessing pipeline applying noise filtering, normalization, and feature extraction to raw sensor data for model inputs."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataValidationService, FeatureExtractionService
]

instance GeometricModelingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "CAD integration engine for creating and manipulating geometric representations of physical components with real-time sensor data overlays."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, DesignValidationService
]

instance ValidationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine for validating data quality and model accuracy using statistical methods and anomaly detection."
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeStateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates best-estimate parameters of the Physical Twin's cyber-physical state using sensor data and state estimation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides StateEstimationInsight
]

    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationAction
]

    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes dynamic models to predict Physical Twin responses to current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictionInsight
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance SupervisoryControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides ControlCommandAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

    DTDFVocab:provides VisualDiagnosticInsight
]

]

    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

    DTDFVocab:provides StateEstimationInsight
]

    DTDFVocab:provides PredictionInsight

    DTDFVocab:provides ConsequenceAnalysisInsight
]

    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight

    DTDFVocab:provides DataValidationInsight

    DTDFVocab:provides FeatureExtractionInsight

    DTDFVocab:provides DesignValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Best-estimate parameters of the Physical Twin's cyber-physical state including force distributions and stress levels derived from sensor fusion."
]

    base:desc "Triggers alerts or corrective actions when system deviations exceed safe operational envelopes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    base:desc "Quantified confidence measures in model predictions derived from calibration algorithms and uncertainty propagation techniques."
]

    base:desc "Predicted responses of the Physical Twin to current or hypothetical operating conditions including collision dynamics and wear progression."
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance PhysicalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment containing external factors influencing Physical Twin behavior including forces, weather conditions, and electromagnetic interference."
    base:contains PhysicalComponent
]

instance CyberEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cyber environment containing control systems, communication networks, and data processing components interacting with the Physical Twin."
    base:contains CyberComponent
]

// C13: Components
instance PhysicalComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical/electrical components of the Physical Twin including chassis, wheels, and actuators with measurable properties for modeling."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalEnvironment
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, PressureSensor, VibrationSensor, PositionSensor, VelocitySensor, ForceSensor, CalibrationSensor
]

instance CyberComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Embedded sensors, controllers, and communication networks enabling data collection and actuation in the Cyber-Physical System."
    base:isContainedIn CyberEnvironment
    base:contains OpcUaGateway, EthernetIpNetwork, MqttBroker, KalmanFilterEngine, ValidationEngine
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in a Digital Twin system enables **closed-loop control**, where the DT generates **commands, optimizations, or alerts** that influence the Physical Twin (PT). Key components include:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Control signals** (e.g., PWM for motor speed, voltage/current adjustments for actuators).
   - **Operational directives** (e.g., start/stop commands, mode switches for CPS).
   - **Maintenance triggers** (e.g., predictive replacement schedules for wear-and-tear components).
   - **Optimization adjustments** (e.g., dynamic reconfiguration of system parameters via **model predictive control (MPC)**).

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Structured protocols** like **OPC UA Information Models** for standardized command payloads.
   - **JSON/XML** for human-readable configuration updates (e.g., parameter tuning).
   - **Binary protocols** (e.g., **CAN bus messages**) for real-time industrial automation.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Digital signatures** and **TLS/SSL encryption** to verify command origin and integrity.
   - **Role-based access control (RBAC)** to restrict command execution to authorized entities (e.g., human operators or trusted algorithms).
   - **Pre-command validation** via **simulation sandboxing** (e.g., running commands in a DT replica to check feasibility before deployment).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Real-time telemetry loops** where PT responses (e.g., sensor data post-command) are fed back into the DT for **adaptive control** or **reinforcement learning**.
   - **Closed-loop optimization** using **reinforcement learning agents** or **adaptive controllers** (e.g., PID tuning via DT simulations).
   - **Human-in-the-loop validation** where operators approve critical commands via **interactive dashboards**.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Priority-based arbitration** (e.g., emergency stops override optimization commands).
   - **Consensus algorithms** (e.g., **Raft** or **Paxos**) for distributed DTs to resolve conflicting control signals from multiple sources.
   - **Fallback mechanisms** (e.g., reverting to last-known-good state if command execution fails).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Acknowledgment protocols** (e.g., **ACK/NACK** messages) to confirm receipt and execution.
   - **Post-command verification** via **sensor data correlation** (e.g., comparing expected vs. actual PT state changes).
   - **Audit logs** stored in **blockchain-like ledgers** (or immutable databases) for traceability of all commands and outcomes.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal synchronization framework that integrates real-time data streams from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, enabling continuous calibration and monitoring. The system employs calibration algorithms to dynamically update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with the best parameter estimates, typically at frequencies dictated by the operational dynamics of the PT (e.g., milliseconds to seconds, depending on the system's criticality). Latency requirements are implied to be low for real-time monitoring and control, ensuring that state estimators, fault detectors, and supervisory controllers can respond promptly to deviations or violations in system performance. Temporal granularity is adaptable to the specific needs of the DT services, ranging from high-frequency data acquisition for dynamic simulations to lower-frequency updates for long-term predictive analytics. The system leverages time-series data to correlate physical events with digital model outputs, ensuring temporal alignment between the PT and its Digital Twin representation for accurate behavior reproduction and prediction under current operating conditions."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a modular architecture that integrates three core components: **Data** (collected from the PT via sensors over time), **Models** (abstract representations of the PT’s cyber and physical aspects, including its environment), and **Algorithms** (technical processes such as fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimizers). These components are orchestrated through a feedback loop where calibration algorithms refine the models using real-time data, enabling dynamic adjustments. The system employs a centralized coordination mechanism to manage interactions between the PT and DT, ensuring that detected violations (e.g., safety or performance deviations) trigger human or automated interventions. The architecture supports hierarchical decision-making, where higher-level controllers oversee system-wide optimality while lower-level algorithms handle real-time adjustments. Resource management is implied to be adaptive, prioritizing critical services (e.g., fault detection) over non-critical ones during operational constraints."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) spans multiple stages, beginning with **conceptualization and design**, where abstract models (e.g., CAD representations of physical components like car chassis or wheel dimensions) are developed to capture specific system attributes (e.g., geometry, material properties). These models are later refined into **dynamic models** (e.g., differential equation-based simulations for analyzing forces and movement dynamics) to enable behavioral analysis.

During the **development phase**, the DT integrates **data collection** from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, **knowledge models** (e.g., cyber-physical system (CPS) and environmental models), and **algorithmic components** (e.g., calibration algorithms, fault detectors, state estimators). The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with real-time parameter estimates, ensuring alignment between the digital and physical representations.

The **operational phase** involves continuous monitoring of the PT’s performance using the DT’s **supervisory controllers** and **safety monitors**, which detect deviations from optimal/safe operating conditions. Violations trigger **adaptive decision-making**, where human operators or automated systems adjust the PT’s parameters or configurations. Transition management between stages relies on **iterative validation loops**, where simulation results are compared against empirical data to refine models iteratively.

Lifecycle governance is implicit in the DT’s **closed-loop architecture**, where models are never discarded post-deployment but instead evolve through **real-time feedback loops**. This ensures continuous alignment with the PT’s evolving state, adhering to principles of **model-driven engineering** and **systems-of-systems integration**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) requires rigorous **fidelity and validity considerations** to ensure models and data accurately represent the **Physical Twin (PT)**. Key components include:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: High fidelity is mandated for **dynamic and analytical models** (e.g., differential equation-based simulations) to replicate PT behavior under operational conditions. Fidelity is achieved through **calibration algorithms** that adjust model parameters using real-time sensor data.
- **Validation and verification methods**: While not explicitly detailed, the document implies **comparative analysis** between simulated and observed PT behavior to validate model accuracy. Verification likely involves **cross-checking** model outputs against empirical data (e.g., sensor readings).
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Uncertainties arise from **sensor noise**, **model simplifications**, and **environmental variations**. Quantification is inferred through **calibration algorithms**, which iteratively refine models to minimize parameter errors.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: The DT employs **monitoring systems** to detect deviations (e.g., safety violations) and trigger **corrective actions** (e.g., human or algorithmic interventions) to align the PT with predicted behavior.
- **Quality assurance processes**: The document emphasizes **continuous integration** of models and data post-deployment, ensuring ongoing validity through **real-time updates** and **iterative refinement** of computational models.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin (DT) Technical Connection** encompasses the **network infrastructure** enabling seamless interaction between the DT and Physical Twin (PT), including:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Industrial protocols**: **OPC UA** (for secure, platform-agnostic communication), **PROFINET** (for Ethernet-based automation), and **Modbus** (for legacy systems).
   - **Cloud/edge protocols**: **MQTT** (for lightweight IoT messaging), **gRPC** (for high-performance RPC), and **WebSockets** (for real-time bidirectional streams).
   - **Wireless protocols**: **5G** (for ultra-low latency <10ms), **LoRaWAN** (for wide-area sensor networks), and **Wi-Fi 6** (for high-bandwidth local connectivity).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical models**:
     - **Edge layer**: Local DT nodes (e.g., PLCs, gateways) processing data in real-time.
     - **Cloud layer**: Centralized DT servers hosting **simulation engines**, **big data analytics**, and **AI/ML models**.
     - **Fog layer**: Intermediate nodes (e.g., micro-data centers) for latency-sensitive applications.
   - **Mesh networks** for redundant, self-healing connectivity in industrial environments.
   - **Virtualized overlays** (e.g., **SDN/NFV**) to dynamically allocate network resources.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Critical paths** (e.g., control loops): <10ms latency, **10–100 Mbps** bandwidth.
   - **Monitoring streams**: <100ms latency, **1–10 Mbps** bandwidth.
   - **Historical data**: Non-real-time, **100 Mbps+** for archival storage.
   - **Simulation workloads**: **GPU-accelerated clusters** with **10 Gbps+** interconnects for physics-based modeling.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Hot standby systems** (e.g., dual DT instances with **synchronous replication**).
   - **Failover protocols** (e.g., **VRRP**, **keepalived**) for seamless switching during network partitions.
   - **Data replication** across **geo-distributed edge/cloud nodes** with **consistency guarantees** (e.g., **Paxos**, **Raft**).
   - **Self-healing networks** using **SDN controllers** to reroute traffic dynamically.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Encryption**: **TLS 1.3** for transport security, **IPsec** for VPN-based isolation.
   - **Authentication**: **OAuth 2.0**, **JWT**, and **X.509 certificates** for identity verification.
   - **Zero-trust architecture**: **Continuous authentication** (e.g., behavioral biometrics) and **micro-segmentation** to limit lateral movement.
   - **Anomaly detection**: **AI-driven intrusion detection systems (IDS)** monitoring network traffic for zero-day attacks.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin (DT) Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure supports the execution of **simulation, analytics, and control algorithms**, with requirements tailored to **scalability, performance, and resilience**. Key components include:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Cloud platforms**: **AWS IoT Greengrass** (for edge-cloud hybrid), **Azure Digital Twins** (pre-built DT framework), **Google Cloud’s Vertex AI** (for ML integration).
   - **Edge platforms**: **NVIDIA Jetson** (for embedded DTs), **Intel OpenVINO** (for optimized inference), **AWS Snowball** (for offline data processing).
   - **On-premise**: **Kubernetes clusters** (for containerized DT microservices), **HPC systems** (for high-fidelity simulations).

2. **Computational Requirements**:
   - **CPU**: Multi-core **x86_64** or **ARM** processors (e.g., **Intel Xeon**, **AMD EPYC**, **Qualcomm Snapdragon 8cx Gen 3** for edge).
   - **GPU**: **NVIDIA Tesla/V100** (for physics simulations), **AMD Instinct** (for AI acceleration).
   - **FPGA/ASIC**: **Xilinx Alveo** (for custom control logic acceleration).
   - **Memory**: **16–128 GB RAM** (depending on model complexity), **NVMe SSDs** for low-latency storage.

3. **Storage Requirements**:
   - **Primary storage**: **High-speed NVMe SSDs** (e.g., **Dell EMC PowerScale**) for real-time data.
   - **Secondary storage**: **Cold storage** (e.g., **AWS S3 Glacier**, **Azure Archive Storage**) for historical data.
   - **Database systems**:
     - **Time-series**: **TimescaleDB**, **InfluxDB** (for sensor data).
     - **Graph databases**: **Neo4j** (for relationship-heavy DT models).
     - **NoSQL**: **MongoDB** (for unstructured metadata).

4. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Horizontal scaling**: **Kubernetes auto-scaling** (e.g., **HPA**, **Cluster Autoscaler**) for DT workloads.
   - **Vertical scaling**: **Elastic cloud instances** (e.g., **AWS EC2 On-Demand/Spot**) for peak loads.
   - **Sharding**: **Partitioning DT models** across nodes (e.g., by geographic region or functional domain).
   - **Serverless**: **AWS Lambda** or **Azure Functions** for event-driven DT components.

5. **Deployment Models**:
   - **Monolithic**: Single DT instance (e.g., **Siemens MindSphere** for industrial use cases).
   - **Microservices**: **Decoupled components** (e.g., **simulation service**, **control service**, **analytics service**) with **REST/gRPC APIs**.
   - **Hybrid cloud-edge**: **Edge DTs** for local processing + **cloud DTs** for global analytics.
   - **Federated DTs**: **Distributed DTs** with **consensus protocols** (e.g., **Hyperledger Fabric**) for collaborative optimization.

6. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **High availability (HA)**: **Multi-AZ deployments** (e.g., **AWS Multi-Region**) with **99.99% uptime SLAs**.
   - **Disaster recovery (DR)**: **Geo-redundant backups** (e.g., **AWS Global Accelerator**) with **RTO < 15 minutes**.
   - **Self-healing**: **Chaos engineering** (e.g., **Gremlin**, **Chaos Mesh**) to test resilience.
   - **Redundant components**: **Active-active failover** for critical DT services (e.g., **PostgreSQL streaming replication**).
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) framework incorporates critical security and safety measures to ensure reliable operation of the Physical Twin (PT). **Cybersecurity protocols** are embedded within the system to protect data integrity and availability, particularly for **sensors and calibration algorithms** that update models of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and their environments. **Access control mechanisms** are implemented to restrict unauthorized modifications to models, ensuring that only authorized entities (humans or algorithms) can intervene when **violations** (e.g., safety or performance deviations) are detected by **monitoring systems**.

**Authentication systems** are utilized to validate the identity of users or automated agents interacting with the DT, preventing unauthorized access to sensitive operational data. **Threat mitigation strategies** include real-time monitoring of system behavior to detect anomalies, with **fail-safe mechanisms** deployed to halt or correct operations if deviations exceed predefined thresholds. **Risk assessment approaches** are applied during model calibration and simulation phases to evaluate potential failures, ensuring compliance with safety-critical system requirements. The framework emphasizes **safety-critical considerations** by integrating **state estimators** and **supervisory controllers** to maintain operational integrity under dynamic conditions, as described in the calibration and monitoring workflows of the DT structure."
]
